title: Invalid cgroup fs type after updating on Fedora 33
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`podman` outputs that I have the wrong filesystem type mounted on `/sys/fs/cgroup`:

```
% stat -f -c %T /sys/fs/cgroup
cgroup2fs
% podman run fedora:33
Error: OCI runtime error: invalid file system type on '/sys/fs/cgroup'
```

See containers/crun#433.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Update to latest Fedora 33
2. No longer works?

**Describe the results you received:**

`podman` says I have the wrong cgroup type enabled.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Running a container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.0-rc1
API Version:  3.2.0-rc1
Go Version:   go1.15.11
Built:        Wed May  5 17:03:47 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Has anyone been able to replicate this since? If not, I think we should close.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I believe this was probably fixed by `podman system migrate` or the like from another issue. I haven't seen it in a while.
